title: ESFEX: Energy System FlEXibility — Power System Optimization
description: ESFEX (Energy System FlEXibility) is an open-source hybrid Python/Julia framework for power system capacity expansion and operational dispatch under high renewable penetration. It couples a strategic capacity expansion planner (Master Problem) with a detailed operational dispatch engine through a two-stage decomposition, so investment decisions are operationally validated (ramp rates, minimum stable generation, storage cycling, demand response, sector coupling) before being accepted. Features include DC and AC optimal power flow, N-1 security, frequency stability, battery storage, sector coupling (electrolyzer/P2H2, primary energy supply chain, EV/V2G, rooftop solar), MGA and SPORES near-optimal exploration, scenario-based stochastic programming, Sobol sensitivity analysis, and a GIS-based Studio. Optimization is implemented in Julia via JuMP and orchestrated from Python through juliacall.
